item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I have no objection to this change. Thanks wikiwalk!

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer removed, author approved edit

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

My proposal is simple. This skip mentions MC&D, so I propose linking to the MC&D hub.

Editing this:

We will be in contact.
~Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd.

To this:

We will be in contact.
~Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not bad, but it's not particularly entertaining, either.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoy this SCP and it's not just for the story, it's the effects and dangers of the object that are so funny to me.

Imagain someone died from too much alcohol without knowing or if someone died because the alcohol either sprayed them in the face too hard or drowned them in it.

This is truly a Drunkard's Dream. This is awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, thank you. It seems like a thing both redneck (substituting an Irish sword for a calvary sabre and a California sparkling wine for a Champagne) and nerdy (sabrage at all), so pretty much as far from cool as is possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was being serious about the being pretty boss thing ;A;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your sarcasm, it wounds me, madam. I shall go console myself with additional low class substitutes for whatever sophisticated people console themselves with. Good day!

:)

(I really have opened a sparkling wine with an arming sword1, FWIW. I have also opened beers with the USMC NCO sword, but the technique is different.)

Footnotes
1. A replica of the sword from the River Suck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sabering champagne (also called sabrage) isn't a necessary part for the Master of Wine, but you do need a comprehensive knowledge of varietals, winemaking, pairing, and basically anything wine.

(and the technique involves a swift strike in an upward motion at the base of the head of the bottle, breaking the lip off)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohh I see.

I only know about Master of Wine because one day I watched a youtube video "how to" for sabering a champagne bottle, and the lady demonstrating how to use the sword/knife/blade was a Master of Wine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes that sounds pretty boss you are cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I have not. I mostly was involved in the business of making wine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have arming sworded a sparkling wine…

Close enough?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAAAA HAVE YOU EVER SABERED A CHAMPAGNE BOTTLE?!

not that I recommend trying, no, do not try at home plz I'm just asking WrongJohnSilver >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I used to work in the wine industry myself, so yes, I know about the Master of Wine and all. Nice to see it mentioned in an SCP!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a footnote. It should be around 1/10 the weight, but if my numbers are super off, someone should check me on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The one quibble I have with this SCP (it's a minor one) involves the lack of a measurement describing exactly (or approximately) how much difference there is between the weight of a liquid outside the bottle and the weight of one inside of the bottle. I would think the Foundation would test that early on, since it's such an easy test to perform

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got what this one was trying to imply, but the story didn't really grab me. It just felt like a D&D magic item with a little bit of backstory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the subject drinks from SCP-2485, they will do so continuously for approximately 45 seconds or until the bottle is emptied. The subject, however, perceives this drink as "a couple of sips" and will usually request to drink more.

Note the pause length in the recording.

Put bluntly...
hide

The friend is drunk out of their mind. They also feel pretty guilty about getting into the Institute because they know the guy is way more talented than them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would the Institute of the Masters of Wine accept someone who can't pronounce pinot and generally doesn't appear to have any knowledge of or interest in oenology? I have no idea what the requirements are IRL but it seems unlikely to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like good old Series 1 awesomeness. It's hard these days to make something so basic, yet so interesting as all those ideas have been taken up. This hits the bullseye however.

You get my vote, Conglaturation !!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also not feeling strongly compelled. I read it because I had nothing better to do, but nothing here really made me feel strongly in any sense. I guess it's a relatively mundane item that has a backstory, certainly, but not of the kind that is really communicated well by the format. The fact that the framing of the story is wine, which I have some degree of idiosyncratic antipathy for and general ignorance regarding, does not help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll see what can be done about the image size, yeah.

Edit: Big thanks to Doctor Cimmerian, who helped resize the image!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm gonna jump onto the "not particularly compelling boat". However, it was still interesting enough to keep my attention and I like the quaintness of it all. Tentative up-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a quick tech gripe… you mind reducing the size of your image file? 2 megs is really excessive for something that gets shrunk to only 300 pixels across. Makes it hard for mobile users and just takes up space otherwise. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
maybe it's just me, but I feel like mcd is reaching awcy levels of shoehorning.

I dunno. I always felt like GOIs would consistently be shoehorned into skips on seemingly random and baseless aspects, just for the purpose of mentioning the GOI. It feels like that kind of practice goes back to before AWCY.

I think the MC&D reference is fine here, but I'm going to have to novote because I don't feel strongly about it one way or the other. It's written fine, just not much interest in it from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An earlier draft version didn't have the MCD reference, but most reviewers preferred the mention. It filled in some of the holes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, there was some brainstorming about just how much meddling MCD was going to do, and the finalized draft had a lot trimmed out. Didn't want to lay on the sad too much, and I really liked the addenda RandomGuy wrote, so that was kept.

Basically the idea eventually evolved to become "feel-good party item that attracts the wrong attention".

All the same, thanks for reading. :)

item.title: Opinions?
item.content:encoded:
So guys, my first SCP. I'd link to thank the aforementioned people for assisting in the creation and Zyn for pretty much changing a simple infinite bottle thing to a noice backstory, and piloting the whole thing. I was just a co-pilot, thing.

Anyways, thoughts? I'd love to hear from you guys

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh… Gonna agree with kaktus, it's just not particularly compelling.

EDIT: maybe it's just me, but I feel like mcd is reaching awcy levels of shoehorning.

item.title: The Photographer
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna lie, this was very exciting. I've only been here about a month and have been so self conscious about my writing and contributions. I was excited when the coauthors liked my ideas, and even more so when they used a picture I took. (Also about that picture, i took it with my phone! I can't believe my phone can take such great pictures.)

This was a really cool experience. Now that my first contribution isn't a total failure, I'll take some more risks with my writing. :P

wish me luck!

Katelyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ayy not bad! :D +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I mentioned in chat, it's not poorly written, just… not terribly exciting. Never felt moved to upvote it, so it's a novote from me.

item.title: Co-author post!
item.content:encoded:
This is a collab between RandomGuy331 and me. Relevant forum thread here.

It’s been lots of fun putting this together. :D

Many, many thanks to the following lovely people:

  • EldritchCyanide, for all-around help and getting other people involved in chat!
  • LadyKatie, for plot discussion (vineyard) and for going through pictures of two bottles and spending 20 minutes soaking off a label so we could have a picture to use!
  • Jacob Conwell for stylistic notes
  • Decibelles for help with story aspects
  • Devereaux for phrasing fixes
  • Wogglebug for specific wordings and details!
  • DolphinSlugchugger for sig figs lol
  • PilotK, JamJam66, Dr_Leonard and kaktus for reading drafts through in chat!

Let me know if I've forgotten to mention anyone.

Will you have spoilers
YES SPOILERS HERE YOU GO

For those interested in the backstory:

  • Guy makes anomalous wine bottles for party funtimes. Guy and friend want to become Masters of Wine.
  • Guy and friend apply to Institute, friend makes it, Guy doesn't. Friend takes one of the anomalous bottles to Institute.
  • Guy receives offer of business from MCD.
  • Guy ignores offer from MCD, MCD shows up and nicks his other anomalous bottles, leave note.
  • Guy leaves home to avoid MCD.
  • Guy receives note and sole remaining bottle from friend, realizes that he was good enough to be a Master all along.
  • Guy goes, "fuck it" and drinks self to oblivion.

Saying no to MCD is hard.

And yes, Masters of Wine is a thing. Just like opening a champage bottle with a saber is a thing (do not try this thing).
